reasoning: The question evaluates whether fatalities in Bangladesh over a 30-day period leading up to the resolution date will exceed the 30-day average over the previous 360 days. The reference mean is 31.167. In fatality data (like ACLED), events are typically over-dispersed and positively skewed. This means the median is lower than the mean, as the mean is frequently elevated by occasional high-fatality outlier events (such as major protests, riots, or unrest). Because a random 30-day sum is drawn from a right-skewed distribution, the probability of it exceeding the long-term mean is statistically less than 0.5. Given standard dispersion levels for political violence data in Bangladesh, the probability of a randomly selected single month exceeding exactly the historical mean is roughly 35-45%. Without strong evidence of an escalating trend that specifically targets the late Dec 2025 - Jan 2026 window, I base the prediction largely on this structural property of the distribution.
